156	def statistical_summary(
157	    data_points: list[dict],
158	    group_by: str = "source_url",
159	) -> dict:
160	    """Aggregate numeric data points across multiple studies.
161	
162	    Args:
163	        data_points: List of dicts with keys: label, value (str or float),
164	            unit, year, source_url, evidence_id.
165	        group_by: Field to group by. Use "source_url" for per-study or
166	            "label" for per-metric aggregation.
167	
168	    Returns:
169	        Dictionary containing:
170	            - markdown_table: Formatted markdown table string
171	            - statistics: Global summary statistics (mean, median, etc.)
172	            - by_group: Per-group statistics
173	            - insights: Auto-generated insight strings
174	    """
175	    if not data_points:
176	        logger.warning(
177	            "[analysis_toolkit] statistical_summary called with empty data_points"
178	        )
179	        return {
180	            "markdown_table": "",
181	            "statistics": {},
182	            "by_group": {},
183	            "insights": ["No data points provided for analysis."],
184	        }
185	
186	    # Enforce data point limit
187	    truncated = data_points[:_MAX_DATA_POINTS]
188	    if len(data_points) > _MAX_DATA_POINTS:
189	        logger.warning(
190	            "[analysis_toolkit] Truncating %d data points to %d",
191	            len(data_points),
192	            _MAX_DATA_POINTS,
193	        )
194	
195	    # Parse numeric values
196	    parsed = []
197	    parse_failures = 0
198	    for dp in truncated:
199	        numeric_val = _safe_float(dp.get("value"))
200	        if numeric_val is not None:
201	            parsed.append({
202	                "label": str(dp.get("label", "")),
203	                "value": numeric_val,
204	                "unit": str(dp.get("unit", "")),
205	                "year": str(dp.get("year", "")),
206	                "source_url": str(dp.get("source_url", "")),
207	                "evidence_id": str(dp.get("evidence_id", "")),
208	                "group_key": str(dp.get(group_by, "ungrouped")),
209	            })
210	        else:
211	            parse_failures += 1
212	
213	    if not parsed:
214	        return {
215	            "markdown_table": "",
216	            "statistics": {},
217	            "by_group": {},
218	            "insights": [
219	                f"All {len(truncated)} data points had non-numeric values "
220	                f"that could not be parsed."
221	            ],
222	        }
223	
224	    if parse_failures > 0:
225	        logger.info(
226	            "[analysis_toolkit] %d/%d data points had non-numeric values (skipped)",
227	            parse_failures,
228	            len(truncated),
229	        )
230	
231	    values = np.array([p["value"] for p in parsed])
232	
233	    # Global statistics
234	    mean_val = float(np.mean(values))
235	    median_val = float(np.median(values))
236	    std_val = float(np.std(values, ddof=1)) if len(values) > 1 else 0.0
237	    min_val = float(np.min(values))
238	    max_val = float(np.max(values))
239	    n = len(values)
240	
241	    # 95% confidence interval using t-distribution
242	    ci_lower = mean_val
243	    ci_upper = mean_val
244	    if n >= _MIN_CI_SAMPLE_SIZE and std_val > 0:
245	        se = std_val / math.sqrt(n)
246	        t_crit = stats.t.ppf((1 + _META_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL) / 2, df=n - 1)
247	        ci_lower = mean_val - t_crit * se
248	        ci_upper = mean_val + t_crit * se
249	
250	    global_stats = {
251	        "mean": round(mean_val, 4),
252	        "median": round(median_val, 4),
253	        "std": round(std_val, 4),
254	        "min": round(min_val, 4),
255	        "max": round(max_val, 4),
256	        "n": n,
257	        "ci_95_lower": round(ci_lower, 4),
258	        "ci_95_upper": round(ci_upper, 4),
259	    }
260	
261	    # Per-group statistics
262	    groups = defaultdict(list)
263	    for p in parsed:
264	        groups[p["group_key"]].append(p["value"])
265	
266	    by_group = {}
267	    for group_key, group_values in groups.items():
268	        gv = np.array(group_values)
269	        g_mean = float(np.mean(gv))
270	        g_std = float(np.std(gv, ddof=1)) if len(gv) > 1 else 0.0
271	        by_group[group_key] = {
272	            "mean": round(g_mean, 4),
273	            "median": round(float(np.median(gv)), 4),
274	            "std": round(g_std, 4),
275	            "min": round(float(np.min(gv)), 4),
276	            "max": round(float(np.max(gv)), 4),
277	            "n": len(gv),
278	        }
279	
280	    # Build markdown table
281	    unit_str = parsed[0]["unit"] if parsed[0]["unit"] else "value"
282	    headers = ["Group", f"Mean ({unit_str})", "Median", "Std Dev", "Min", "Max", "N"]
283	    rows = []
284	    for group_key in sorted(by_group.keys()):
285	        gs = by_group[group_key]
286	        display_key = (
287	            _extract_source_label(group_key) if group_by == "source_url"
288	            else group_key
289	        )
290	        rows.append([
291	            display_key,
292	            _format_number(gs["mean"]),
293	            _format_number(gs["median"]),
294	            _format_number(gs["std"]),
295	            _format_number(gs["min"]),
296	            _format_number(gs["max"]),
297	            str(gs["n"]),
298	        ])
299	
300	    # Add summary row
301	    rows.append([
302	        "**Overall**",
303	        f"**{_format_number(mean_val)}**",
304	        f"**{_format_number(median_val)}**",
305	        f"**{_format_number(std_val)}**",
306	        f"**{_format_number(min_val)}**",
307	        f"**{_format_number(max_val)}**",
308	        f"**{n}**",
309	    ])
310	
311	    markdown_table = _build_markdown_table(headers, rows)
312	
313	    # Generate insights
314	    insights = []
315	    insights.append(
316	        f"Values range from {_format_number(min_val)} to {_format_number(max_val)} "
317	        f"({unit_str}) across {n} data points from {len(groups)} sources."
318	    )
319	
320	    if n >= _MIN_CI_SAMPLE_SIZE and std_val > 0:
321	        insights.append(
322	            f"The mean is {_format_number(mean_val)} "
323	            f"(95% CI: {_format_number(ci_lower)} to {_format_number(ci_upper)})."
324	        )
325	
326	    cv = (std_val / abs(mean_val) * 100) if mean_val != 0 else 0
327	    if cv > 50:
328	        insights.append(
329	            f"High variability detected (CV = {_format_number(cv)}%). "
330	            f"Results differ substantially across sources."
331	        )
332	    elif cv > 20:
333	        insights.append(
334	            f"Moderate variability (CV = {_format_number(cv)}%). "
335	            f"Some disagreement between sources."
336	        )
337	    else:
338	        insights.append(
339	            f"Low variability (CV = {_format_number(cv)}%). "
340	            f"Sources show good agreement."
341	        )
342	
343	    if parse_failures > 0:
344	        insights.append(
345	            f"Note: {parse_failures} data points had non-numeric values and were excluded."
346	        )
347	
348	    return {
349	        "markdown_table": markdown_table,
350	        "statistics": global_stats,
351	        "by_group": by_group,
352	        "insights": insights,
353	    }

356	def _sanitize_column_header(raw: str, max_len: int = 40) -> str:
357	    """FIX-D2: Truncate long column headers to readable length.
358	
359	    Tries to split at natural break points before falling back to
360	    hard truncation.
361	    """
362	    if len(raw) <= max_len:
363	        return raw
364	    for sep in [",", " - ", ": ", " ("]:
365	        pos = raw.find(sep)
366	        if 10 < pos < max_len:
367	            return raw[:pos]
368	    return raw[:max_len - 3] + "..."

371	def build_comparison_table(
372	    data_points: list[dict],
373	    row_field: str = "source_url",
374	    col_field: str = "label",
375	    value_field: str = "value",
376	) -> str:
377	    """Build a markdown pivot/comparison table from structured data.
378	
379	    Creates a pivot table where rows = studies/sources, columns = metrics,
380	    and cells = values. Handles missing values with a dash. Appends a
381	    summary row with mean/range for each column.
382	
383	    Args:
384	        data_points: List of dicts with at least the row_field, col_field,
385	            and value_field keys.
386	        row_field: Field to use for row grouping (default: source_url).
387	        col_field: Field to use for column headers (default: label).
388	        value_field: Field containing the cell values (default: value).
389	
390	    Returns:
391	        Markdown table string. Empty string if insufficient data.
392	    """
393	    if not data_points:
394	        return ""
395	
396	    truncated = data_points[:_MAX_DATA_POINTS]
397	
398	    # Build pivot structure
399	    row_keys = []
400	    col_keys = []
401	    seen_rows = set()
402	    seen_cols = set()
403	    pivot = defaultdict(dict)
404	
405	    for dp in truncated:
406	        row_val = str(dp.get(row_field, ""))
407	        col_val = str(dp.get(col_field, ""))
408	        cell_val = dp.get(value_field, "")
409	
410	        if not row_val or not col_val:
411	            continue
412	
413	        if row_val not in seen_rows:
414	            row_keys.append(row_val)
415	            seen_rows.add(row_val)
416	        if col_val not in seen_cols:
417	            col_keys.append(col_val)
418	            seen_cols.add(col_val)
419	
420	        # If multiple values for same cell, keep the latest
421	        pivot[row_val][col_val] = str(cell_val) if cell_val is not None else "\u2014"
422	
423	    if not row_keys or not col_keys:
424	        return ""
425	
426	    # Build headers — FIX-D2: sanitize long column headers
427	    row_header_label = row_field.replace("_", " ").title()
428	    headers = [row_header_label] + [
429	        _sanitize_column_header(c) for c in col_keys
430	    ]
431	    expected_cols = len(headers)
432	
433	    # Build data rows — FIX-D2: pad/truncate to match header count
434	    rows = []
435	    for row_key in row_keys:
436	        display_key = (
437	            _extract_source_label(row_key) if row_field == "source_url"
438	            else row_key
439	        )
440	        row_data = [display_key]
441	        for col_key in col_keys:
442	            cell = pivot[row_key].get(col_key, "\u2014")
443	            row_data.append(cell)
444	        # Pad short rows
445	        while len(row_data) < expected_cols:
446	            row_data.append("\u2014")
447	        # Truncate long rows
448	        row_data = row_data[:expected_cols]
449	        rows.append(row_data)
450	
451	    # Add summary row (mean/range for numeric columns, count for non-numeric)
452	    summary_row = ["**Summary**"]
453	    for col_key in col_keys:
454	        col_values = []
455	        for row_key in row_keys:
456	            raw = pivot[row_key].get(col_key)
457	            if raw is not None:
458	                numeric = _safe_float(raw)
459	                if numeric is not None:
460	                    col_values.append(numeric)
461	
462	        if col_values:
463	            col_mean = np.mean(col_values)
464	            col_min = min(col_values)
465	            col_max = max(col_values)
466	            if col_min == col_max:
467	                summary_row.append(f"**{_format_number(col_mean)}**")
468	            else:
469	                summary_row.append(
470	                    f"**{_format_number(col_mean)}** "
471	                    f"({_format_number(col_min)}\u2013{_format_number(col_max)})"
472	                )
473	        else:
474	            reporting_count = sum(
475	                1 for rk in row_keys if col_key in pivot[rk]
476	            )
477	            summary_row.append(f"{reporting_count}/{len(row_keys)} reported")
478	
479	    rows.append(summary_row)
480	
481	    return _build_markdown_table(headers, rows)

483	
484	def detect_outliers(
485	    values: list[float],
486	    method: str = "iqr",
487	) -> dict:
488	    """Detect outliers using IQR or Z-score method.
489	
490	    Args:
491	        values: List of numeric values to analyze.
492	        method: Detection method. One of "iqr" (Interquartile Range)
493	            or "zscore" (Z-score). Default: "iqr".
494	
495	    Returns:
496	        Dictionary with:
497	            - outlier_indices: Indices of outlier values
498	            - outlier_values: The outlier values themselves
499	            - bounds: (lower_bound, upper_bound) tuple
500	            - method: Method used
501	    """
502	    empty_result = {
503	        "outlier_indices": [],
504	        "outlier_values": [],
505	        "bounds": (0.0, 0.0),
506	        "method": method,
507	    }
508	
509	    if not values or len(values) < 3:
510	        return empty_result
511	
512	    arr = np.array(values, dtype=float)
513	
514	    # Remove NaN/inf
515	    valid_mask = np.isfinite(arr)
516	    if not np.any(valid_mask):
517	        return empty_result
518	
519	    if method == "zscore":
520	        mean_val = float(np.mean(arr[valid_mask]))
521	        std_val = float(np.std(arr[valid_mask], ddof=1))
522	
523	        if std_val == 0:
524	            return {
525	                "outlier_indices": [],
526	                "outlier_values": [],
527	                "bounds": (mean_val, mean_val),
528	                "method": method,
529	            }
530	
531	        z_scores = np.abs((arr - mean_val) / std_val)
532	        lower_bound = mean_val - _ZSCORE_THRESHOLD * std_val
533	        upper_bound = mean_val + _ZSCORE_THRESHOLD * std_val
534	
535	        outlier_mask = (z_scores > _ZSCORE_THRESHOLD) & valid_mask
536	    else:
537	        # IQR method (default)
538	        q1 = float(np.percentile(arr[valid_mask], 25))
539	        q3 = float(np.percentile(arr[valid_mask], 75))
540	        iqr = q3 - q1
541	
542	        lower_bound = q1 - _IQR_MULTIPLIER * iqr
543	        upper_bound = q3 + _IQR_MULTIPLIER * iqr
544	
545	        outlier_mask = ((arr < lower_bound) | (arr > upper_bound)) & valid_mask
546	
547	    outlier_indices = [int(i) for i in np.where(outlier_mask)[0]]
548	    outlier_values = [float(arr[i]) for i in outlier_indices]
549	
550	    return {
551	        "outlier_indices": outlier_indices,
552	        "outlier_values": outlier_values,
553	        "bounds": (round(lower_bound, 4), round(upper_bound, 4)),
554	        "method": method,
555	    }
556	
557	
558	def compute_agreement_score(evidence_statements: list[str]) -> dict:
559	    """Compute how well evidence sources agree using word overlap Jaccard similarity.
560	
561	    Performs pairwise comparison of all statements using set-based Jaccard
562	    similarity on word tokens. High agreement means sources report similar
563	    information. Low agreement suggests conflicting or complementary evidence.
564	
565	    Args:
566	        evidence_statements: List of text statements to compare.
567	
568	    Returns:
569	        Dictionary with:
570	            - agreement_score: Float 0-1 (mean pairwise Jaccard similarity)
571	            - pairwise_scores: List of all pairwise similarity scores
572	            - consensus_strength: Human-readable label ("strong", "moderate",
573	              "weak", "no consensus")
574	    """
575	    empty_result = {
576	        "agreement_score": 0.0,
577	        "pairwise_scores": [],
578	        "consensus_strength": "insufficient data",
579	    }
580	
581	    if not evidence_statements or len(evidence_statements) < _MIN_AGREEMENT_STATEMENTS:
582	        return empty_result
583	
584	    # Tokenize: lowercase, split on non-alphanumeric, remove stopwords
585	    stopwords = {
586	        "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
587	        "have", "has", "had", "do", "does", "did", "will", "would", "could",
588	        "should", "may", "might", "can", "shall", "to", "of", "in", "for",
589	        "on", "with", "at", "by", "from", "as", "into", "through", "during",
590	        "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "under", "again",
591	        "further", "then", "once", "and", "but", "or", "nor", "not", "so",
592	        "yet", "both", "each", "few", "more", "most", "other", "some",
593	        "such", "no", "only", "own", "same", "than", "too", "very",
594	        "this", "that", "these", "those", "it", "its", "they", "them",
595	        "their", "we", "our", "he", "she", "his", "her", "who", "which",
596	        "what", "when", "where", "how", "all", "any", "if", "also",
597	    }
598	
599	    def tokenize(text: str) -> set[str]:
600	        words = re.findall(r'[a-z0-9]+', text.lower())
601	        return {w for w in words if w not in stopwords and len(w) > 2}
602	
603	    token_sets = [tokenize(stmt) for stmt in evidence_statements]
604	
605	    # Filter out empty token sets
606	    valid_pairs = [
607	        (i, j)
608	        for i, j in combinations(range(len(token_sets)), 2)
609	        if token_sets[i] and token_sets[j]
610	    ]
611	
612	    if not valid_pairs:
613	        return empty_result
614	
615	    pairwise_scores = []
616	    for i, j in valid_pairs:
617	        intersection = len(token_sets[i] & token_sets[j])
618	        union = len(token_sets[i] | token_sets[j])
619	        jaccard = intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0
620	        pairwise_scores.append(round(jaccard, 4))
621	
622	    agreement = float(np.mean(pairwise_scores))
623	
624	    # Classify consensus strength
625	    if agreement >= 0.5:
626	        consensus = "strong"
627	    elif agreement >= 0.3:
628	        consensus = "moderate"
629	    elif agreement >= 0.15:
630	        consensus = "weak"
631	    else:
632	        consensus = "no consensus"
633	
634	    return {
635	        "agreement_score": round(agreement, 4),
636	        "pairwise_scores": pairwise_scores,
637	        "consensus_strength": consensus,
638	    }
639	
640	
641	def rank_evidence_by_impact(data_points: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
642	    """Rank data points by how much they deviate from the group mean.
643	
644	    High-impact data points are outliers that contradict or significantly
645	    extend the consensus. Each data point receives an impact_score (absolute
646	    z-score) and an impact_reason explaining why it is notable.
647	
648	    Args:
649	        data_points: List of dicts with at least a "value" key. Values
650	            are parsed to float; non-numeric entries receive impact_score 0.
651	
652	    Returns:
653	        Sorted list of data_points (highest impact first) with added
654	        "impact_score" and "impact_reason" fields. Original dicts are
655	        not mutated; new dicts are returned.
656	    """
657	    if not data_points:
658	        return []
659	
660	    truncated = data_points[:_MAX_DATA_POINTS]
661	
662	    # Parse values
663	    enriched = []
664	    numeric_values = []
665	
666	    for dp in truncated:
667	        new_dp = dict(dp)
668	        numeric_val = _safe_float(dp.get("value"))
669	        new_dp["_parsed_value"] = numeric_val
670	        if numeric_val is not None:
671	            numeric_values.append(numeric_val)
672	        enriched.append(new_dp)
673	
674	    if not numeric_values:
675	        # No numeric values: assign zero impact to all
676	        for dp in enriched:
677	            dp["impact_score"] = 0.0
678	            dp["impact_reason"] = "Non-numeric value; could not assess impact."
679	            dp.pop("_parsed_value", None)
680	        return enriched
681	
682	    mean_val = float(np.mean(numeric_values))
683	    std_val = float(np.std(numeric_values, ddof=1)) if len(numeric_values) > 1 else 0.0
684	
685	    for dp in enriched:
686	        parsed = dp.pop("_parsed_value", None)
687	
688	        if parsed is None:
689	            dp["impact_score"] = 0.0
690	            dp["impact_reason"] = "Non-numeric value; could not assess impact."
691	            continue
692	
693	        if std_val == 0:
694	            dp["impact_score"] = 0.0
695	            dp["impact_reason"] = "All numeric values are identical; no deviation."
696	            continue
697	
698	        z = abs(parsed - mean_val) / std_val
699	        dp["impact_score"] = round(z, 4)
700	
701	        # Classify the deviation
702	        direction = "above" if parsed > mean_val else "below"
703	        deviation_pct = abs(parsed - mean_val) / abs(mean_val) * 100 if mean_val != 0 else 0
704	
705	        if z >= _HIGH_IMPACT_THRESHOLD:
706	            dp["impact_reason"] = (
707	                f"High impact: value {_format_number(parsed)} is "
708	                f"{_format_number(deviation_pct)}% {direction} the mean "
709	                f"({_format_number(mean_val)}), z-score = {_format_number(z)}."
710	            )
711	        elif z >= 1.0:
712	            dp["impact_reason"] = (
713	                f"Moderate impact: value {_format_number(parsed)} is "
714	                f"{_format_number(deviation_pct)}% {direction} the mean "
715	                f"({_format_number(mean_val)})."
716	            )
717	        else:
718	            dp["impact_reason"] = (
719	                f"Low impact: value {_format_number(parsed)} is near the mean "
720	                f"({_format_number(mean_val)})."
721	            )
722	
723	    # Sort by impact_score descending
724	    enriched.sort(key=lambda dp: dp.get("impact_score", 0.0), reverse=True)
725	
726	    return enriched
727	
728	
729	def generate_meta_analysis_summary(data_points: list[dict]) -> str:
730	    """Generate a mini meta-analysis summary across studies.
731	
732	    Computes pooled mean weighted by inverse variance, heterogeneity via
733	    the I-squared statistic approximation, and produces a forest-plot-style
734	    markdown table showing each study's contribution.
735	
736	    Args:
737	        data_points: List of dicts with keys: label (study name), value
738	            (numeric), unit, source_url, evidence_id. Multiple data points
739	            from the same source_url are averaged into one study estimate.
740	
741	    Returns:
742	        Markdown string containing the forest-plot table and a statistical
743	        narrative. Empty string if insufficient data.
744	    """
745	    if not data_points:
746	        return ""
747	
748	    truncated = data_points[:_MAX_DATA_POINTS]
749	
750	    # Parse and group by source (each source = one "study")
751	    study_data = defaultdict(list)
752	    unit_str = ""
753	
754	    for dp in truncated:
755	        numeric_val = _safe_float(dp.get("value"))
756	        if numeric_val is None:
757	            continue
758	        source_key = str(dp.get("source_url", dp.get("label", "Unknown")))
759	        study_data[source_key].append(numeric_val)
760	        if not unit_str:
761	            unit_str = str(dp.get("unit", ""))
762	
763	    if len(study_data) < 2:
764	        return (
765	            "Insufficient studies for meta-analysis "
766	            f"(need >= 2, found {len(study_data)})."
767	        )
768	
769	    # Compute per-study mean and standard error
770	    studies = []
771	    for source_key, vals in study_data.items():
772	        arr = np.array(vals)
773	        study_mean = float(np.mean(arr))
774	        study_n = len(vals)
775	        # Standard error: use within-study std if n > 1, else estimate from
776	        # overall data spread
777	        if study_n > 1:
778	            study_se = float(np.std(arr, ddof=1) / math.sqrt(study_n))
779	        else:
780	            study_se = None  # Will be imputed below
781	        studies.append({
782	            "source": source_key,
783	            "label": _extract_source_label(source_key),
784	            "mean": study_mean,
785	            "se": study_se,
786	            "n": study_n,
787	        })
788	
789	    # Impute SE for single-observation studies using median SE of others
790	    known_ses = [s["se"] for s in studies if s["se"] is not None and s["se"] > 0]
791	    if known_ses:
792	        imputed_se = float(np.median(known_ses))
793	    else:
794	        # All studies have single observations; use pooled std estimate
795	        all_means = [s["mean"] for s in studies]
796	        imputed_se = float(np.std(all_means, ddof=1) / math.sqrt(len(all_means)))
797	        if imputed_se == 0:
798	            imputed_se = 1.0  # Prevent division by zero
799	
800	    for study in studies:
801	        if study["se"] is None or study["se"] == 0:
802	            study["se"] = imputed_se
803	
804	    # Inverse-variance weighting
805	    weights = []
806	    for study in studies:
807	        w = 1.0 / (study["se"] ** 2) if study["se"] > 0 else 0.0
808	        study["weight"] = w
809	        weights.append(w)
810	
811	    total_weight = sum(weights)
812	    if total_weight == 0:
813	        return "Cannot compute pooled estimate (all weights are zero)."
814	
815	    # Normalize weights to percentages
816	    for study in studies:
817	        study["weight_pct"] = (study["weight"] / total_weight) * 100
818	
819	    # Pooled mean (fixed-effects)
820	    pooled_mean = sum(s["mean"] * s["weight"] for s in studies) / total_weight
821	    pooled_se = math.sqrt(1.0 / total_weight) if total_weight > 0 else 0.0
822	
823	    # Confidence interval
824	    alpha = 1 - _META_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL
825	    z_crit = stats.norm.ppf(1 - alpha / 2)
826	    pooled_ci_lower = pooled_mean - z_crit * pooled_se
827	    pooled_ci_upper = pooled_mean + z_crit * pooled_se
828	
829	    # Heterogeneity: Cochran's Q and I-squared
830	    q_stat = sum(
831	        s["weight"] * (s["mean"] - pooled_mean) ** 2 for s in studies
832	    )
833	    df = len(studies) - 1
834	    i_squared = max(0.0, (q_stat - df) / q_stat * 100) if q_stat > 0 else 0.0
835	
836	    # Per-study CI for forest plot
837	    for study in studies:
838	        ci_half = z_crit * study["se"]
839	        study["ci_lower"] = study["mean"] - ci_half
840	        study["ci_upper"] = study["mean"] + ci_half
841	
842	    # Sort by weight descending
843	    studies.sort(key=lambda s: s["weight_pct"], reverse=True)
844	
845	    # Build forest-plot-style markdown table
846	    unit_label = f" ({unit_str})" if unit_str else ""
847	    headers = [
848	        "Study",
849	        f"Estimate{unit_label}",
850	        "95% CI",
851	        "Weight (%)",
852	        "N",
853	    ]
854	
855	    rows = []
856	    for study in studies:
857	        rows.append([
858	            study["label"],
859	            _format_number(study["mean"]),
860	            f"{_format_number(study['ci_lower'])} \u2013 {_format_number(study['ci_upper'])}",
861	            _format_number(study["weight_pct"], 1),
862	            str(study["n"]),
863	        ])
864	
865	    # Pooled row
866	    rows.append([
867	        "**Pooled estimate**",
868	        f"**{_format_number(pooled_mean)}**",
869	        f"**{_format_number(pooled_ci_lower)} \u2013 {_format_number(pooled_ci_upper)}**",
870	        "**100.0**",
871	        f"**{sum(s['n'] for s in studies)}**",
872	    ])
873	
874	    table = _build_markdown_table(headers, rows)
875	
876	    # Statistical narrative
877	    lines = [
878	        table,
879	        "",
880	        f"**Pooled estimate (fixed-effects):** "
881	        f"{_format_number(pooled_mean)}{unit_label} "
882	        f"(95% CI: {_format_number(pooled_ci_lower)} to "
883	        f"{_format_number(pooled_ci_upper)})",
884	        "",
885	    ]
886	
887	    # Heterogeneity interpretation
888	    if i_squared < 25:
889	        het_label = "low"
890	    elif i_squared < 50:
891	        het_label = "moderate"
892	    elif i_squared < 75:
893	        het_label = "substantial"
894	    else:
895	        het_label = "considerable"
896	
897	    lines.append(
898	        f"**Heterogeneity:** Q = {_format_number(q_stat)}, "
899	        f"df = {df}, I\u00b2 = {_format_number(i_squared, 1)}% ({het_label})"
900	    )
901	
902	    if i_squared >= 50:
903	        lines.append(
904	            f"Note: {het_label} heterogeneity (I\u00b2 = "
905	            f"{_format_number(i_squared, 1)}%) suggests results vary "
906	            f"substantially across studies. A random-effects model may be "
907	            f"more appropriate."
908	        )
909	
910	    lines.append(
911	        f"\n*Based on {len(studies)} studies with "
912	        f"{sum(s['n'] for s in studies)} total observations.*"
913	    )
914	
915	    return "\n".join(lines)

1	"""
2	Sandboxed Python code execution for research data analysis.
3	
4	Executes LLM-generated Python analysis scripts in an isolated subprocess
5	with timeout enforcement, import restrictions, and output size limits.
6	Scripts receive data via JSON stdin, produce results via JSON stdout,
7	and may generate matplotlib figures returned as base64 PNG.
8	
9	This is what makes POLARIS competitive with Claude Code: the LLM can
10	write arbitrary Python analysis scripts that run on real evidence data,
11	producing custom statistical analyses, charts, and tables.
12	"""
13	
14	import ast

27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# LAW VI: All configuration from environment variables
32	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	
34	_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_CODE_EXEC_TIMEOUT", "30"))
35	_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE = int(os.getenv("PG_CODE_EXEC_MAX_OUTPUT", "1000000"))  # 1MB
36	_MAX_SCRIPT_SIZE = int(os.getenv("PG_CODE_EXEC_MAX_SCRIPT", "50000"))  # 50KB

192	
193	# I-f10-007: AST-level validators close the regex bypasses (comma-separated
194	# imports, indirect __builtins__ access, frame reflection, numpy ctypeslib,
195	# ctypes attribute chains). Acknowledged: complete sovereignty requires
196	# OS-level isolation per follow-up Issue I-f10-007b.
197	
198	_BLOCKED_REFLECTION_ATTRS = frozenset({
199	    # __builtins__ and direct builtin access
200	    "__builtins__",
201	    "__import__",
202	    "__class__",
203	    "__subclasses__",
204	    "__globals__",
205	    "__bases__",
206	    "__mro__",
207	    "__dict__",
208	    "__getattribute__",
209	    "__getattr__",
210	    "__base__",
211	    # Frame reflection paths
212	    "_getframe",
213	    "f_builtins",
214	    "f_globals",
215	    "f_locals",
216	    "f_code",
217	    "gi_frame",
218	    "cr_frame",
219	    "ag_frame",
220	    "tb_frame",
221	    # numpy/scipy native FFI escape vectors
222	    "ctypeslib",
223	    "ctypes",
224	    # sys.modules subscript escape
225	    "modules",
226	})
227	
228	
229	def _validate_ast(script: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
230	    """AST-based validation: import allowlist + reflection-attribute blocklist.
231	
232	    Closes the regex bypasses found in Codex iter-1..5 review:
233	    - comma-separated imports (`import json, socket`)
234	    - indirect __builtins__ reflection
235	    - frame-reflection (`sys._getframe().f_builtins`)
236	    - numpy.ctypeslib FFI escape
237	
238	    Residual surface (documented in I-f10-007b follow-up):
239	    - dangerous builtins as first-class values: `list(map(eval, ...))`
240	    - `__class__.__bases__[0].__subclasses__()` chain (mitigated by
241	      blocking `__subclasses__` attr but the `__class__` chain start is
242	      caught too)
243	    """
244	    try:
245	        tree = ast.parse(script)
246	    except SyntaxError as exc:
247	        return False, f"Script syntax error: {str(exc)[:200]}"
248	
249	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
250	        # Import allowlist enforcement
251	        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
252	            for alias in node.names:
253	                top_module = alias.name.split(".")[0]
254	                if top_module in _BLOCKED_IMPORTS:
255	                    return False, (
256	                        f"Blocked import '{top_module}' "
257	                        f"(security restriction)"
258	                    )
259	                if top_module not in _ALLOWED_IMPORTS:
260	                    return False, (
261	                        f"Module '{top_module}' is not in the allowed "
262	                        f"import list (security restriction)"
263	                    )
264	        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
265	            if node.module:
266	                top_module = node.module.split(".")[0]
267	                if top_module in _BLOCKED_IMPORTS:
268	                    return False, (
269	                        f"Blocked import 'from {top_module}' "
270	                        f"(security restriction)"
271	                    )
272	                if top_module not in _ALLOWED_IMPORTS:
273	                    return False, (
274	                        f"Module 'from {top_module}' is not in the "
275	                        f"allowed import list (security restriction)"
276	                    )
277	        # Direct __builtins__ name reference
278	        elif isinstance(node, ast.Name) and node.id in _BLOCKED_REFLECTION_ATTRS:
279	            return False, (
280	                f"Reference to '{node.id}' is not allowed "
281	                f"(reflection-bypass guard)"
282	            )
283	        # Attribute access matching the blocked reflection set
284	        elif isinstance(node, ast.Attribute) and node.attr in _BLOCKED_REFLECTION_ATTRS:
285	            return False, (
286	                f"Attribute access to '.{node.attr}' is not allowed "
287	                f"(reflection-bypass guard)"
288	            )
289	        # I-f10-007 iter-3 diff fix: reject Name references (not just Call)
290	        # to dangerous reflection/exec builtins. This catches first-class
291	        # aliasing: `e = eval; e('...')`, `v = vars; v(sys)['modules']`, etc.
292	        # AST `ast.Name` in Load context covers both bare reference and call.
293	        elif isinstance(node, ast.Name) and node.id in {
294	            "vars", "dir", "globals", "locals",
295	            "eval", "exec", "open", "compile",
296	            "getattr", "setattr", "delattr",
297	        }:
298	            return False, (
299	                f"Reference to dangerous builtin '{node.id}' is not allowed "
300	                f"(reflection/exec-bypass guard)"
301	            )
302	
303	    return True, ""
304	
305	
306	def validate_script(script: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
307	    """Check a script for blocked imports and dangerous patterns.
308	
309	    Performs static analysis only -- does not execute the script. Checks
310	    import statements against the blocklist and scans for dangerous
311	    built-in function calls.
312	
313	    Args:
314	        script: Python source code to validate.
315	
316	    Returns:
317	        Tuple of (is_safe, reason). is_safe is True if the script passes
318	        all checks. reason explains the first violation found (empty
319	        string when safe).
320	    """
321	    if not script or not script.strip():
322	        return False, "Script is empty"
323	
324	    if len(script) > _MAX_SCRIPT_SIZE:
325	        return False, (
326	            f"Script exceeds maximum size ({len(script)} > {_MAX_SCRIPT_SIZE} bytes)"
327	        )
328	
329	    # I-f10-007: AST validation runs first; closes comma-separated import,
330	    # __builtins__ reflection, frame-reflection, and ctypeslib bypasses.
331	    ast_ok, ast_reason = _validate_ast(script)
332	    if not ast_ok:
333	        return False, ast_reason
334	
335	    # Normalize line continuations for analysis
336	    normalized = script.replace("\\\n", " ")
337	
338	    # Check each line for import violations
339	    # Split semicolon-separated statements into separate lines
340	    raw_lines = normalized.split("\n")
341	    expanded_lines = []
342	    for raw_line in raw_lines:
343	        expanded_lines.extend(raw_line.split(";"))
344	
345	    for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(expanded_lines, start=1):
346	        line = raw_line.strip()
347	
348	        # Skip comments and empty lines
349	        if not line or line.startswith("#"):
350	            continue
351	
352	        # Check "import X" and "from X import Y"
353	        import_match = re.match(
354	            r'(?:from\s+(\S+)\s+import|import\s+(\S+))', line
355	        )
356	        if import_match:
357	            module_name = import_match.group(1) or import_match.group(2)
358	            # Strip trailing punctuation (semicolons, commas)
359	            module_name = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9_.]', '', module_name)
360	            # Check the top-level module (e.g., "os" from "os.path")
361	            top_module = module_name.split(".")[0]
362	            if top_module in _BLOCKED_IMPORTS:
363	                return False, (
364	                    f"Line {line_num}: Blocked import '{top_module}' "
365	                    f"(security restriction)"
366	                )
367	
368	    # Check for dangerous built-in patterns
369	    for pattern, reason in _DANGEROUS_PATTERNS:
370	        match = re.search(pattern, normalized)
371	        if match:
372	            # Check if the match is inside a comment or string
373	            # (rough heuristic: find the line containing the match)
374	            match_pos = match.start()
375	            preceding = normalized[:match_pos]
376	            line_start = preceding.rfind("\n") + 1
377	            line_text = normalized[line_start:].split("\n")[0].strip()
378	
379	            # Skip if the pattern appears inside a comment
380	            comment_pos = line_text.find("#")
381	            match_in_line = match.start() - line_start
382	            if comment_pos >= 0 and match_in_line > comment_pos:
383	                continue
384	
385	            # Skip if inside a string literal (very rough check)
386	            before_match = line_text[:match_in_line]
387	            single_quotes = before_match.count("'") - before_match.count("\\'")
388	            double_quotes = before_match.count('"') - before_match.count('\\"')
389	            if single_quotes % 2 == 1 or double_quotes % 2 == 1:
390	                continue
391	
392	            return False, f"Dangerous pattern detected: {reason}"
393	
394	    return True, ""
395

401	async def execute_analysis_script(
402	    script: str,
403	    input_data: dict | None = None,
404	    timeout: int | None = None,
405	) -> dict:
406	    """Execute a Python analysis script in a sandboxed subprocess.
407	
408	    The script runs in a fresh Python process with a temp working directory.
409	    Data is passed via stdin as JSON. Results are expected as JSON on stdout.
410	    Matplotlib figures embedded as base64 PNG in the JSON output are extracted
411	    into the "charts" field.
412	
413	    The subprocess environment includes SANDBOX_READ_DIR and SANDBOX_WRITE_DIR
414	    pointing to controlled directories for evidence file access and result output.
415	
416	    Args:
417	        script: Python code to execute. Must print JSON to stdout.
418	        input_data: Dict passed to the script via stdin as JSON. The script
419	            reads it with ``json.load(sys.stdin)``.
420	        timeout: Max execution time in seconds. Defaults to PG_CODE_EXEC_TIMEOUT.
421	
422	    Returns:
423	        Dictionary with:
424	            - success: Whether execution completed without errors
425	            - stdout: Raw stdout content
426	            - stderr: Raw stderr content
427	            - result: Parsed JSON from stdout (None on failure)
428	            - charts: List of extracted chart dicts with base64 images
429	            - execution_time_seconds: Wall-clock execution time
430	            - error: Error description (None on success)
431	    """
432	    effective_timeout = timeout if timeout is not None else _TIMEOUT
433	
434	    empty_result = {
435	        "success": False,
436	        "stdout": "",
437	        "stderr": "",
438	        "result": None,
439	        "charts": [],
440	        "execution_time_seconds": 0.0,
441	        "error": None,
442	    }
443	
444	    # Validate the script first
445	    is_safe, safety_reason = validate_script(script)
446	    if not is_safe:
447	        logger.warning(
448	            "[code_executor] Script validation failed: %s", safety_reason
449	        )
450	        return {**empty_result, "error": f"Validation failed: {safety_reason}"}
451	
452	    # I-f10-007: runtime socket monkey-patch preamble injected before user
453	    # script. Even when allowlisted libraries (pandas/numpy/etc.) attempt
454	    # network egress via socket APIs, the patched callable raises and the
455	    # call fails. This is defense-in-depth on top of validate_script;
456	    # complete sovereignty still requires OS-level isolation per
457	    # I-f10-007b follow-up.
458	    socket_kill_preamble = (
459	        "def _polaris_block(*args, **kwargs):\n"
460	        "    raise RuntimeError('network egress blocked by polaris sandbox')\n"
461	        "import socket as _polaris_socket\n"
462	        "for _polaris_attr in ('socket','create_connection','getaddrinfo',\n"
463	        "                     'gethostbyname','gethostbyname_ex','gethostbyaddr',\n"
464	        "                     'create_server','fromfd','socketpair','SocketType'):\n"
465	        "    setattr(_polaris_socket, _polaris_attr, _polaris_block)\n"
466	        "try:\n"
467	        "    import _socket as _polaris_socket_raw\n"
468	        "    for _polaris_attr in ('socket','SocketType','getaddrinfo',\n"
469	        "                          'gethostbyname','gethostbyname_ex',\n"
470	        "                          'gethostbyaddr','fromfd','socketpair'):\n"
471	        "        try:\n"
472	        "            setattr(_polaris_socket_raw, _polaris_attr, _polaris_block)\n"
473	        "        except (AttributeError, TypeError):\n"
474	        "            pass\n"
475	        "    del _polaris_socket_raw\n"
476	        "except ImportError:\n"
477	        "    pass\n"
478	        # Remove sandbox-internal names from user-visible globals.
479	        "del _polaris_socket\n"
480	        "del _polaris_attr\n"
481	    )
482	    script = socket_kill_preamble + script
483	
484	    # Unconditionally enforce Agg backend -- prevents Windows Tk display hangs
485	    agg_preamble = "import matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use('Agg')\n"
486	    if "matplotlib" in script and "matplotlib.use(" not in script:
487	        script = agg_preamble + script
488	
489	    # Prepare stdin data
490	    stdin_json = ""
491	    if input_data is not None:
492	        try:
493	            stdin_json = json.dumps(input_data, default=str)
494	        except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
495	            return {
496	                **empty_result,
497	                "error": f"Failed to serialize input_data: {str(exc)[:200]}",
498	            }
499	        if len(stdin_json) > _MAX_INPUT_DATA_SIZE:
500	            return {
501	                **empty_result,
502	                "error": (
503	                    f"Input data exceeds maximum size "
504	                    f"({len(stdin_json)} > {_MAX_INPUT_DATA_SIZE} bytes)"
505	                ),
506	            }
507	
508	    # Write script and input to temp files
509	    script_path = None
510	    input_path = None
511	    work_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="polaris_exec_")
512	
513	    try:
514	        # Write script
515	        script_path = os.path.join(work_dir, "analysis_script.py")
516	        with open(script_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
517	            f.write(script)
518	
519	        # Write input data if provided
520	        if stdin_json:
521	            input_path = os.path.join(work_dir, "input_data.json")
522	            with open(input_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
523	                f.write(stdin_json)
524	
525	    except Exception as exc:
526	        logger.error(
527	            "[code_executor] Failed to write temp files: %s", str(exc)[:200]
528	        )
529	        _cleanup_temp(work_dir, script_path, input_path)
530	        return {
531	            **empty_result,
532	            "error": f"Failed to write temp files: {str(exc)[:200]}",
533	        }
534	
535	    # Execute in subprocess
536	    start_time = time.monotonic()
537	
538	    try:
539	        # Build command
540	        cmd = [sys.executable, "-u", script_path]
541	
542	        # Prepare stdin
543	        stdin_bytes = stdin_json.encode("utf-8") if stdin_json else None
544	
545	        # Run in a thread to avoid blocking the event loop
546	        proc_result = await asyncio.wait_for(
547	            asyncio.to_thread(
548	                _run_subprocess,
549	                cmd=cmd,
550	                stdin_data=stdin_bytes,
551	                timeout=effective_timeout,
552	                cwd=work_dir,
553	            ),
554	            timeout=effective_timeout + 5,  # Grace period for thread overhead
555	        )
556	
557	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
558	
559	        if proc_result["returncode"] != 0:
560	            stderr_preview = proc_result["stderr"][:1000] if proc_result["stderr"] else ""
561	            logger.warning(
562	                "[code_executor] Script failed (exit=%d, %.1fs): %s",
563	                proc_result["returncode"],
564	                elapsed,
565	                stderr_preview,
566	            )
567	            return {
568	                "success": False,
569	                "stdout": proc_result["stdout"][:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE],
570	                "stderr": proc_result["stderr"][:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE],
571	                "result": None,
572	                "charts": [],
573	                "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
574	                "error": f"Script exited with code {proc_result['returncode']}: {stderr_preview}",
575	            }
576	
577	        # Parse stdout
578	        raw_stdout = proc_result["stdout"]
579	        if len(raw_stdout) > _MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE:
580	            logger.warning(
581	                "[code_executor] Output truncated from %d to %d bytes",
582	                len(raw_stdout),
583	                _MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE,
584	            )
585	            raw_stdout = raw_stdout[:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE]
586	
587	        if not raw_stdout.strip():
588	            return {
589	                "success": False,
590	                "stdout": "",
591	                "stderr": proc_result["stderr"][:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE],
592	                "result": None,
593	                "charts": [],
594	                "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
595	                "error": "Script produced no stdout output",
596	            }
597	
598	        # Parse JSON result
599	        try:
600	            parsed = json.loads(raw_stdout.strip())
601	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
602	            # Try to extract JSON from mixed output (script may have print
603	            # statements before the JSON)
604	            parsed = _extract_json_from_output(raw_stdout)
605	            if parsed is None:
606	                return {
607	                    "success": False,
608	                    "stdout": raw_stdout[:2000],
609	                    "stderr": proc_result["stderr"][:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE],
610	                    "result": None,
611	                    "charts": [],
612	                    "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
613	                    "error": f"Invalid JSON output: {str(exc)[:200]}",
614	                }
615	
616	        # Extract charts (base64 PNG images)
617	        charts = _extract_charts(parsed)
618	
619	        logger.info(
620	            "[code_executor] Script completed successfully in %.1fs: "
621	            "%d chars output, %d charts",
622	            elapsed,
623	            len(raw_stdout),
624	            len(charts),
625	        )
626	
627	        return {
628	            "success": True,
629	            "stdout": raw_stdout,
630	            "stderr": proc_result["stderr"][:_MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE],
631	            "result": parsed,
632	            "charts": charts,
633	            "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
634	            "error": None,
635	        }
636	
637	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
638	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
639	        logger.warning(
640	            "[code_executor] Script timed out after %.1fs (limit=%ds)",
641	            elapsed,
642	            effective_timeout,
643	        )
644	        return {
645	            **empty_result,
646	            "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
647	            "error": f"Script timed out after {effective_timeout}s",
648	        }
649	
650	    except Exception as exc:
651	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
652	        logger.error(
653	            "[code_executor] Unexpected error: %s", str(exc)[:300]
654	        )
655	        return {
656	            **empty_result,
657	            "execution_time_seconds": round(elapsed, 3),
658	            "error": f"Execution error: {str(exc)[:300]}",
659	        }
660	
661	    finally:
662	        _cleanup_temp(work_dir, script_path, input_path)

669	async def generate_and_execute_analysis(
670	    client: OpenRouterClient,
671	    evidence_data: list[dict],
672	    analysis_question: str,
673	    research_context: str = "",
674	) -> dict:
675	    """Ask the LLM to write a Python analysis script, then execute it.
676	
677	    This is the "Claude Code for research" function. The LLM sees the
678	    evidence data, writes a custom analysis script tailored to the question,
679	    and we execute it in a sandboxed subprocess.
680	
681	    On execution failure, the error is fed back to the LLM for one retry
682	    attempt with corrective instructions.
683	
684	    Args:
685	        client: OpenRouter LLM client for script generation.
686	        evidence_data: Structured data to analyze (list of evidence dicts).
687	        analysis_question: What to analyze, e.g. "Compare removal efficiency
688	            across studies".
689	        research_context: Topic context for better script generation.
690	
691	    Returns:
692	        Same structure as execute_analysis_script with an additional
693	        "generated_script" field containing the Python code that was run.
694	    """
695	    if not evidence_data:
696	        logger.warning(
697	            "[code_executor] generate_and_execute_analysis called with "
698	            "empty evidence_data"
699	        )
700	        return {
701	            "success": False,
702	            "stdout": "",
703	            "stderr": "",
704	            "result": None,
705	            "charts": [],
706	            "execution_time_seconds": 0.0,
707	            "error": "No evidence data provided",
708	            "generated_script": "",
709	        }
710	
711	    # Build data preview for the LLM prompt
712	    preview_items = evidence_data[:_DATA_PREVIEW_ITEMS]
713	    preview_json = json.dumps(preview_items, indent=2, default=str)
714	    if len(preview_json) > _DATA_PREVIEW_MAX_CHARS:
715	        preview_json = preview_json[:_DATA_PREVIEW_MAX_CHARS] + "\n... (truncated)"
716	
717	    # Describe the data schema from the first item
718	    schema_description = ""
719	    if evidence_data:
720	        sample = evidence_data[0]
721	        schema_lines = []
722	        for key, val in sample.items():
723	            val_type = type(val).__name__
724	            val_preview = str(val)[:80]
725	            schema_lines.append(f"  - {key} ({val_type}): e.g. \"{val_preview}\"")
726	        schema_description = "\n".join(schema_lines)
727	
728	    # Generate the script
729	    script = await _generate_script(
730	        client=client,
731	        evidence_data=evidence_data,
732	        preview_json=preview_json,
733	        schema_description=schema_description,
734	        analysis_question=analysis_question,
735	        research_context=research_context,
736	        error_feedback=None,
737	    )
738	
739	    if not script:
740	        return {
741	            "success": False,
742	            "stdout": "",
743	            "stderr": "",
744	            "result": None,
745	            "charts": [],
746	            "execution_time_seconds": 0.0,
747	            "error": "LLM failed to generate a valid script",
748	            "generated_script": "",
749	        }
750	
751	    # Execute the script
752	    result = await execute_analysis_script(
753	        script=script,
754	        input_data={"data": evidence_data, "question": analysis_question},
755	    )
756	    result["generated_script"] = script
757	
758	    # Retry once on failure with error feedback
759	    if not result["success"] and _MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS > 0:
760	        error_msg = result.get("error", "") or result.get("stderr", "")
761	        logger.info(
762	            "[code_executor] First execution failed, retrying with error feedback: %s",
763	            str(error_msg)[:200],
764	        )
765	
766	        retry_script = await _generate_script(
767	            client=client,
768	            evidence_data=evidence_data,
769	            preview_json=preview_json,
770	            schema_description=schema_description,
771	            analysis_question=analysis_question,
772	            research_context=research_context,
773	            error_feedback=str(error_msg)[:2000],
774	        )
775	
776	        if retry_script:
777	            retry_result = await execute_analysis_script(
778	                script=retry_script,
779	                input_data={"data": evidence_data, "question": analysis_question},
780	            )
781	            retry_result["generated_script"] = retry_script
782	
783	            if retry_result["success"]:
784	                logger.info("[code_executor] Retry succeeded")
785	                return retry_result
786	
787	            # Both attempts failed; return the retry result with both scripts
788	            logger.warning(
789	                "[code_executor] Retry also failed: %s",

791	            )
792	            retry_result["generated_script"] = (

794	                f"# === ATTEMPT 2 (failed) ===\n{retry_script}"

805	def _run_subprocess(
806	    cmd: list[str],
807	    stdin_data: bytes | None,
808	    timeout: int,
809	    cwd: str,
810	) -> dict:
811	    """Run a subprocess synchronously (called via asyncio.to_thread).
812	
813	    Returns dict with returncode, stdout, stderr strings.
814	    """
815	    import subprocess
816	
817	    try:
818	        proc = subprocess.run(
819	            cmd,
820	            input=stdin_data,
821	            capture_output=True,
822	            timeout=timeout,
823	            cwd=cwd,
824	            # Restrict environment: only pass essential variables + sandbox paths
825	            env=_build_restricted_env(),
826	        )
827	        return {
828	            "returncode": proc.returncode,
829	            "stdout": proc.stdout.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"),
830	            "stderr": proc.stderr.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"),
831	        }
832	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
833	        return {
834	            "returncode": -1,
835	            "stdout": "",
836	            "stderr": f"Process killed after {timeout}s timeout",
837	        }
838	    except Exception as exc:
839	        return {
840	            "returncode": -1,
841	            "stdout": "",
842	            "stderr": f"Process error: {str(exc)[:500]}",
843	        }

846	def _build_restricted_env() -> dict[str, str]:
847	    """Build a restricted environment for the subprocess.
848	
849	    Passes only the variables needed for Python and data analysis libraries
850	    to function. No API keys, no network configuration.
851	
852	    Includes SANDBOX_READ_DIR and SANDBOX_WRITE_DIR for controlled file
853	    access to evidence files and analysis output.
854	    """
855	    env = {}
856	
857	    # Python needs these to find its stdlib and packages
858	    for key in ("PATH", "PYTHONPATH", "PYTHONHOME", "SYSTEMROOT", "TEMP", "TMP",
859	                "VIRTUAL_ENV", "CONDA_PREFIX", "USERPROFILE", "HOME",
860	                "APPDATA", "LOCALAPPDATA"):
861	        val = os.environ.get(key)
862	        if val is not None:
863	            env[key] = val
864	
865	    # Matplotlib needs a writable config dir
866	    env["MPLCONFIGDIR"] = tempfile.gettempdir()
867	
868	    # Prevent matplotlib from trying to use a GUI backend
869	    env["MPLBACKEND"] = "Agg"
870	
871	    # NumPy/OpenBLAS thread control (prevent fork bombs)
872	    env["OMP_NUM_THREADS"] = "1"
873	    env["MKL_NUM_THREADS"] = "1"
874	    env["OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS"] = "1"
875	
876	    # Sandbox file I/O directories for scripts that need evidence access
877	    sandbox = _prepare_sandbox_env()
878	    env["SANDBOX_READ_DIR"] = sandbox["SANDBOX_READ_DIR"]
879	    env["SANDBOX_WRITE_DIR"] = sandbox["SANDBOX_WRITE_DIR"]
880	
881	    return env

884	def _extract_json_from_output(raw_output: str) -> dict | None:
885	    """Try to extract valid JSON from output that may contain non-JSON lines.
886	
887	    Scripts sometimes print debug output before the JSON. This function
888	    scans for the last JSON object or array in the output.
889	    """
890	    # Try to find the last { ... } block
891	    lines = raw_output.strip().split("\n")
892	
893	    # Strategy 1: Try parsing from the last line that starts with "{"
894	    for i in range(len(lines) - 1, -1, -1):
895	        stripped = lines[i].strip()
896	        if stripped.startswith("{"):
897	            candidate = "\n".join(lines[i:])
898	            try:
899	                return json.loads(candidate)
900	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
901	                continue
902	
903	    # Strategy 2: Find the outermost { } pair
904	    first_brace = raw_output.find("{")
905	    last_brace = raw_output.rfind("}")
906	    if first_brace >= 0 and last_brace > first_brace:
907	        candidate = raw_output[first_brace:last_brace + 1]
908	        try:
909	            return json.loads(candidate)
910	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
911	            pass
912	
913	    return None

917	    """Extract chart data (base64 PNG images) from parsed script output.
918

920	    "image_base64" data. Validates that base64 data decodes to valid PNG.
921	
922	    Returns list of {"title": str, "image_base64": str} dicts.
923	    """
924	    charts = []
925	
926	    if isinstance(parsed_result, dict):
927	        raw_charts = parsed_result.get("charts", [])
928	    elif isinstance(parsed_result, list):
929	        raw_charts = parsed_result
930	    else:
931	        return charts
932	
933	    if not isinstance(raw_charts, list):
934	        return charts
935	
936	    for chart in raw_charts:
937	        if not isinstance(chart, dict):
938	            continue
939	
940	        image_b64 = chart.get("image_base64", "")
941	        if not image_b64 or not isinstance(image_b64, str):
942	            continue
943	
944	        # Validate PNG magic bytes
945	        try:
946	            decoded = base64.b64decode(image_b64)

950	                    chart.get("title", "unknown"),

954	                logger.warning(

956	                    chart.get("title", "unknown"),

963	                chart.get("title", "unknown"),

997	def _extract_code_block(llm_response: str) -> str:
998	    """Extract Python code from an LLM response that may contain markdown fences.
999	
1000	    Handles: ```python ... ```, ```py ... ```, ``` ... ```, or raw code.
1001	    If multiple code blocks exist, returns the largest one.
1002	    """
1003	    # Try to find fenced code blocks
1004	    pattern = r'```(?:python|py)?\s*\n(.*?)```'
1005	    matches = re.findall(pattern, llm_response, re.DOTALL)
1006	
1007	    if matches:
1008	        # Return the largest code block (most likely the actual script)
1009	        return max(matches, key=len).strip()
1010	
1011	    # No fences found; check if the entire response looks like Python code
1012	    stripped = llm_response.strip()
1013	    if stripped.startswith("import ") or stripped.startswith("# ") or stripped.startswith("from "):
1014	        return stripped
1015	
1016	    # Try removing any leading/trailing non-code text
1017	    lines = stripped.split("\n")
1018	    code_lines = []
1019	    in_code = False
1020	    for line in lines:
1021	        if (
1022	            line.strip().startswith(("import ", "from ", "def ", "class ", "#"))
1023	            or line.strip() == ""
1024	            or re.match(r'^\s', line)
1025	        ):
1026	            in_code = True
1027	        if in_code:
1028	            code_lines.append(line)
1029	
1030	    if code_lines:
1031	        return "\n".join(code_lines).strip()
1032	
1033	    return stripped

83	async def _generate_analysis_script(
84	    client: OpenRouterClient,
85	    data_points: list[dict],
86	    analysis_type: str,
87	    research_context: str = "",
88	) -> str:
89	    """Ask Qwen to write a Python analysis script for the data.
90	
91	    The script must output JSON to stdout with charts as base64 images.
92	    """
93	    # Limit data preview to prevent prompt overflow
94	    preview = json.dumps(data_points[:20], indent=2, default=str)
95	    if len(preview) > 5000:
96	        preview = preview[:5000] + "\n... (truncated)"
97	
98	    prompt = f"""Write a Python script that analyzes this data and outputs JSON results.
99	
100	Data ({len(data_points)} points):
101	{preview}
102	
103	Analysis type: {analysis_type}
104	Context: {research_context}
105	
106	Requirements:
107	- Import json, sys, io, base64
108	- Import pandas as pd, numpy as np, matplotlib
109	- Set matplotlib.use('Agg') BEFORE importing pyplot
110	- Import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
111	- Parse the data from a JSON string embedded in the script
112	- Analyze the data: compute statistics, identify trends, rank entities, detect outliers
113	- Generate 1-2 matplotlib charts as base64 PNG images:
114	  - Use white background, clear labels, readable font sizes
115	  - Add proper axis labels and a descriptive title
116	  - Use {PG_CHART_DPI} DPI, max width {PG_CHART_MAX_WIDTH}px
117	  - Save to BytesIO, encode as base64
118	- Output ONLY valid JSON to stdout (no other print statements):
119	  {{"charts": [{{"title": "...", "image_base64": "...", "description": "..."}}],
120	   "tables": [{{"headers": ["col1", "col2"], "rows": [["val1", "val2"]], "caption": "..."}}],
121	   "insights": ["key finding 1", "key finding 2"]}}
122	- Handle missing/inconsistent data gracefully with try/except
123	- Do NOT use plt.show() — only save to buffer
124	"""
125	    system = (
126	        "You are a data analyst writing clean Python scripts. "
127	        "Output ONLY the Python code, no markdown fences, no explanations. "
128	        "The script must be self-contained and executable."
129	    )
130	
131	    response = await client.generate(
132	        prompt=prompt,
133	        system=system,
134	        max_tokens=4096,
135	        temperature=0.3,
136	    )
137	
138	    script = response.content.strip()
139	
140	    # Strip markdown code fences if present (handles ```python, ```py, whitespace)
141	    lines = script.split("\n")
142	    if lines and re.match(r'^\s*```', lines[0]):
143	        lines = lines[1:]  # Remove opening fence
144	        # Remove closing fence if present
145	        if lines and re.match(r'^\s*```\s*$', lines[-1]):
146	            lines = lines[:-1]
147	        script = "\n".join(lines)
148	
149	    # FIX-3B: Strip markdown-formatted lines that LLM may inject as comments.
150	    # These look like: "* **Label:** text", "*   Configure..." or "- **Note:**"
151	    # which are not valid Python and cause SyntaxError.
152	    cleaned_lines = []
153	    in_string = False
154	    for line in script.split("\n"):
155	        stripped = line.strip()
156	        # Track triple-quote strings to avoid false positives
157	        tq_count = stripped.count('"""') + stripped.count("'''")
158	        if tq_count % 2 == 1:
159	            in_string = not in_string
160	        if in_string:
161	            cleaned_lines.append(line)
162	            continue
163	        # Skip markdown bullet lines: * text, - **text**, etc.
164	        # But NOT valid Python like `*args` or `-value`
165	        if re.match(r'^\*\s{2,}', stripped):  # "* " with 2+ spaces = markdown bullet
166	            continue
167	        if re.match(r'^[*\-]\s+\*\*', stripped):  # "* **Bold**" or "- **Bold**"
168	            continue
169	        if re.match(r'^\*\*\w+', stripped):  # "**Bold" at line start
170	            continue
171	        if re.match(r'^[*\-]\s+[A-Z]', stripped) and '=' not in stripped and '(' not in stripped:
172	            # Markdown bullet with Capital letter start, no assignment or call
173	            continue
174	        cleaned_lines.append(line)
175	    script = "\n".join(cleaned_lines)
176	
177	    return script

180	async def _execute_analysis_script(script: str) -> dict:
181	    """Execute a Python analysis script and parse its JSON output.
182	
183	    Uses subprocess for isolation. The script writes JSON to stdout.
184	
185	    Args:
186	        script: Complete Python script as a string.
187	
188	    Returns:
189	        Parsed JSON output from the script, or empty result on failure.
190	    """
191	    empty_result = {"charts": [], "tables": [], "insights": []}
192	
193	    # FIX-1: Unconditionally enforce Agg backend — LLM may omit it,
194	    # causing Windows subprocess hangs when Tk tries to open a display.
195	    agg_preamble = "import matplotlib\nmatplotlib.use('Agg')\n"
196	    if "matplotlib.use(" not in script:
197	        script = agg_preamble + script
198	
199	    # Write script to temp file
200	    try:
201	        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
202	            mode="w",
203	            suffix=".py",
204	            delete=False,
205	            encoding="utf-8",
206	        ) as f:
207	            f.write(script)
208	            script_path = f.name
209	    except Exception as exc:
210	        logger.error(
211	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Failed to write temp script: %s",
212	            str(exc)[:200],
213	        )
214	        return empty_result
215	
216	    try:
217	        # Run in subprocess with timeout
218	        proc = await asyncio.to_thread(
219	            subprocess.run,
220	            [sys.executable, script_path],
221	            capture_output=True,
222	            text=True,
223	            timeout=PG_ANALYSIS_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT,
224	            cwd=tempfile.gettempdir(),
225	        )
226	
227	        if proc.returncode != 0:
228	            logger.warning(
229	                "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Analysis script failed "
230	                "(exit=%d): stderr=%s",
231	                proc.returncode,
232	                proc.stderr[:500] if proc.stderr else "no stderr",
233	            )
234	            return empty_result
235	
236	        # Parse JSON output
237	        stdout = proc.stdout.strip()
238	        if not stdout:
239	            logger.warning(
240	                "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Script produced no stdout output"
241	            )
242	            return empty_result
243	
244	        result = json.loads(stdout)
245	
246	        # Validate structure
247	        charts = result.get("charts", [])
248	        tables = result.get("tables", [])
249	        insights = result.get("insights", [])
250	
251	        logger.info(
252	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Analysis complete: "
253	            "%d charts, %d tables, %d insights",
254	            len(charts), len(tables), len(insights),
255	        )
256	
257	        return {
258	            "charts": charts,
259	            "tables": tables,
260	            "insights": insights,
261	        }
262	
263	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
264	        logger.warning(
265	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Script timed out after %ds",
266	            PG_ANALYSIS_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT,
267	        )
268	        return empty_result
269	
270	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
271	        logger.warning(
272	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Script output is not valid JSON: %s",
273	            str(exc)[:200],
274	        )
275	        return empty_result
276	
277	    except Exception as exc:
278	        logger.warning(
279	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH 2A: Script execution failed: %s",
280	            str(exc)[:200],
281	        )
282	        return empty_result
283	
284	    finally:
285	        # Clean up temp file
286	        try:
287	            os.unlink(script_path)
288	        except OSError:
289	            pass

315	    # breaks rendering and DOCX export.

318	        if not decoded[:4] == b'\x89PNG':

343	def format_table_markdown(
344	    table: dict,
345	    table_number: int,
346	) -> str:
347	    """Format structured data as a markdown table.
348	
349	    Args:
350	        table: {"headers": [str], "rows": [[str]], "caption": str}
351	        table_number: Sequential table number.
352	
353	    Returns:
354	        Markdown table string.
355	    """
356	    headers = table.get("headers", [])
357	    rows = table.get("rows", [])
358	    caption = table.get("caption", f"Table {table_number}")
359	
360	    if not headers or not rows:
361	        return ""
362	
363	    # Build markdown table
364	    lines = []
365	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(str(h) for h in headers) + " |")
366	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join("---" for _ in headers) + " |")
367	    for row in rows:
368	        # Pad or truncate row to match header count
369	        padded = list(row) + [""] * (len(headers) - len(row))
370	        lines.append("| " + " | ".join(str(v) for v in padded[:len(headers)]) + " |")
371	
372	    lines.append(f"\n*Table {table_number}: {caption}*\n")
373	
374	    return "\n".join(lines)
